rawText:
The American dollar has shed roughly a tenth of its value since early 2025, and the implications stretch far beyond currency trading desks. Gold has surged to around $5,000 an ounce, climbing approximately 75 percent in a single year. These two signals together suggest that global investors are growing uneasy about the stability of dollar-denominated assets. Erratic trade policy, ballooning budget deficits, and mounting questions about central-bank independence have combined to undermine the confidence that once made the greenback an unquestioned safe haven.

Beneath the surface, the composition of foreign holdings in America has changed dramatically. Two decades ago, central-bank reserves and sovereign debt purchases dominated foreign inflows, providing a steady, safety-driven foundation. Today, profit-seeking equity investment accounts for a record share, nearly three-fifths, of all foreign-owned American assets. That shift matters because equities are far more sensitive to sentiment. When doubts emerge about the direction of policy or the sustainability of lofty stock valuations, capital can reverse course quickly. Recent episodes illustrate the danger: during tariff-related market shocks, stocks, bonds, and the dollar all fell simultaneously, a pattern once associated mainly with developing economies. Meanwhile, the yield advantage that American government bonds hold over their peers in other major economies has narrowed sharply, reducing one of the strongest incentives for foreign capital to stay.

This rebalancing away from dollar assets is already creating winners elsewhere. Emerging-market equities rose 34 percent in 2025, outpacing developed-world benchmarks by a wide margin, and the momentum has carried into 2026. Currencies from Latin America to Southeast Asia have strengthened. Several forces are at work. A weaker dollar eases the burden of dollar-denominated debts carried by developing nations and supports commodity exports priced in the currency. At the same time, many middle-income countries have spent years strengthening institutions, building foreign-exchange reserves, and granting central banks greater autonomy, making them more attractive destinations for diversifying investors.

The structural case for this rotation is also compelling. Emerging-market stocks trade at a roughly 40 percent discount to their American counterparts, yet they offer exposure to many of the same growth themes, including artificial intelligence, through firms in East Asia. Global growth forecasts show developing economies expanding notably faster than advanced ones, and monetary conditions look supportive: interest rates are expected to decline without a recession on the horizon. For investors who spent years concentrated in a handful of American technology giants, the combination of cheaper valuations and stronger institutional resilience abroad presents a persuasive argument for broader diversification.

None of this means the dollar will lose its status as the world's primary reserve currency overnight. No rival asset is positioned to replace it. However, the greenback's dominance increasingly depends on the continued outperformance of American assets rather than on the structural advantages that once seemed permanent. If large institutional investors continue to hedge their dollar exposure or redirect capital toward other markets, a self-reinforcing cycle could develop: a falling currency reduces the weight of American holdings in global benchmarks, prompting further selling and additional weakness. The lesson from previous periods of dollar decline is clear: once that cycle begins, it can persist for years.

overview.title: The Weakening Dollar and the Global Investment Shift

sentences.original: The American dollar has shed roughly a tenth of its value since early 2025, and the implications stretch far beyond currency trading desks.
sentences.translation: 미국 달러는 2025년 초 이후 가치의 약 10분의 1을 잃었으며, 그 영향은 환율 거래 데스크를 훨씬 넘어 뻗어나간다.

grammarElements.text: roughly a tenth of its value
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: and the implications stretch far beyond currency trading desks
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The stock shed nearly twenty percent of its value in a single week.
The ruling has broad implications for the entire industry.

expressions.usage: 어떤 영향이나 범위가 예상보다 훨씬 넓게 확장됨을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has shed [fraction] of its [value], and the implications stretch far beyond [immediate domain]
patterns.explanation: 수치적 변화를 제시한 뒤 그 파급효과가 특정 분야를 넘어 광범위함을 강조하는 도입 구조
examples: The pound has shed a fifth of its purchasing power, and the implications stretch far beyond import bills.
The index has shed a quarter of its peak value, and the implications stretch far beyond Wall Street.

drillQuestions.question: The American dollar has ______ roughly a tenth of its value since early 2025.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입문. shed는 불규칙 동사(shed-shed-shed)로 '잃다'라는 뜻이며, 일반적인 lose보다 저널리즘적 문체에서 선호된다. a tenth는 분수 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: has shed roughly a tenth of its value

sentences.original: Gold has surged to around $5,000 an ounce, climbing approximately 75 percent in a single year.
sentences.translation: 금은 온스당 약 5,000달러까지 급등하여, 단 1년 만에 약 75% 상승했다.

grammarElements.text: climbing approximately 75 percent in a single year
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 부가 설명

vocabulary.example: Oil prices surged after the supply disruption was announced.
Gold is commonly quoted in dollars per troy ounce.

expressions.usage: 짧은 기간 내의 극적인 변화를 강조하는 시간 표현. single이 '겨우'라는 뉘앙스를 더한다

patterns.pattern: [Asset] has surged to [price level], climbing [percentage] in [time period]
patterns.explanation: 자산의 절대 가격을 제시한 뒤 분사구문으로 상승률과 기간을 부가 설명하는 경제 보도 패턴
examples: Bitcoin has surged to eighty thousand dollars, climbing forty percent in a single quarter.
Copper has surged to record levels, climbing thirty percent in six months.

drillQuestions.question: Gold has ______ to around $5,000 an ounce.

sentences.notes: 현재완료(has surged)가 최근까지의 누적 상승을, 분사구문(climbing)이 구체적 폭을 보충 설명한다. $5,000이라는 수치 자체가 충격 효과를 노린 배치.

sentences.original: These two signals together suggest that global investors are growing uneasy about the stability of dollar-denominated assets.
sentences.translation: 이 두 신호는 함께, 글로벌 투자자들이 달러 표시 자산의 안정성에 대해 점점 불안해하고 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: that global investors are growing uneasy about the stability of dollar-denominated assets
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어

vocabulary.example: Most international trade is conducted in dollar-denominated contracts.
Markets grew uneasy after the central bank's ambiguous statement.

expressions.usage: 감정이 점진적으로 변화하고 있음을 나타내는 진행형 표현. grow는 become의 문어적 대안

patterns.pattern: These [number] signals together suggest that [subject] is growing [adjective] about [concern]
patterns.explanation: 복수의 증거를 종합하여 하나의 결론을 이끌어내는 분석적 서술 구조
examples: These three indicators together suggest that consumers are growing cautious about spending.
These data points together suggest that the recovery is growing fragile.

drillQuestions.question: Global investors are growing uneasy about the stability of dollar-______ assets.

sentences.notes: together가 두 신호를 결합하는 역할을 하며, growing uneasy는 grow + 형용사 구문(점점 ~해지다)으로 변화의 진행을 표현한다.

sentences.original: Erratic trade policy, ballooning budget deficits, and mounting questions about central-bank independence have combined to undermine the confidence that once made the greenback an unquestioned safe haven.
sentences.translation: 변덕스러운 무역 정책, 급팽창하는 재정 적자, 그리고 중앙은행 독립성에 대한 가중되는 의문이 결합하여, 한때 달러를 의심의 여지 없는 안전 자산으로 만들었던 신뢰를 약화시켰다.

grammarElements.text: Erratic trade policy, ballooning budget deficits, and mounting questions about central-bank independence
grammarElements.label: 삼중 병렬 주어

grammarElements.text: that once made the greenback an unquestioned safe haven
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (과거 회상)

vocabulary.example: The erratic behavior of the stock market unnerved investors.
Ballooning debt levels have raised alarm among economists.
The greenback weakened against a basket of major currencies.

expressions.usage: 복수의 요인이 함께 작용하여 부정적 결과를 초래했음을 나타내는 표현
불확실한 시기에 투자자들이 자금을 옮기는 안전한 자산을 가리키는 금융 용어

patterns.pattern: [Cause A], [Cause B], and [Cause C] have combined to undermine [foundation] that once [verb-ed] [subject] [status]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 원인을 병렬로 나열한 뒤 과거의 확고한 지위가 훼손되었음을 대조하는 구조
examples: Rising costs, regulatory pressure, and shifting demand have combined to undermine the dominance that once made the firm an industry leader.
Corruption, inflation, and emigration have combined to erode the stability that once made the nation a regional anchor.

drillQuestions.question: Erratic trade policy, ballooning budget deficits, and mounting questions have combined to ______ the confidence.

sentences.notes: 첫 문단의 핵심 분석문. 삼중 병렬(erratic, ballooning, mounting)이 달러 신뢰 약화의 다면적 원인을 열거하며, 각 형용사가 점진적 강도를 높인다. greenback은 달러의 저널리즘적 별칭.

koreanAlignment.en: the greenback an unquestioned safe haven
koreanAlignment.ko: 달러를 의심의 여지 없는 안전 자산

sentences.original: Beneath the surface, the composition of foreign holdings in America has changed dramatically.
sentences.translation: 표면 아래에서, 미국 내 외국인 보유 자산의 구성은 극적으로 변화했다.

grammarElements.text: the composition of foreign holdings in America
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: The composition of the workforce has shifted toward service industries.
Foreign holdings of American debt have declined for three straight years.

expressions.meaning: 표면 아래에서, 겉으로 드러나지 않는 곳에서
expressions.usage: 눈에 보이지 않는 구조적 변화를 소개할 때 사용하는 은유적 도입 표현

patterns.pattern: Beneath the surface, [subject] has changed dramatically
patterns.explanation: 표면적으로 보이지 않는 근본적 변화를 독자에게 환기시키는 문단 도입 구조
examples: Beneath the surface, the structure of global trade has changed dramatically.
Beneath the surface, the balance of political power has changed dramatically.

drillQuestions.question: Beneath the surface, the ______ of foreign holdings in America has changed dramatically.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 도입문. Beneath the surface라는 은유가 독자에게 '보이지 않는 구조적 변화에 주목하라'고 신호를 보낸다.

sentences.original: Two decades ago, central-bank reserves and sovereign debt purchases dominated foreign inflows, providing a steady, safety-driven foundation.
sentences.translation: 20년 전에는 중앙은행 외환보유고와 국채 매입이 외국 자본 유입을 지배하여, 안정적이고 안전 추구형 기반을 제공했다.

grammarElements.text: central-bank reserves and sovereign debt purchases
grammarElements.label: 복합 주어

vocabulary.example: Sovereign debt levels in southern Europe raised concerns about default risk.
Capital inflows into emerging markets reached a record high.

expressions.usage: 하이픈 복합 형용사로, 투자 동기가 수익이 아닌 안전성임을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [Time marker] ago, [X and Y] dominated [domain], providing [quality] foundation
patterns.explanation: 과거의 안정적 상태를 제시하여 현재와의 대비를 준비하는 시간 대조 패턴
examples: A decade ago, fixed-income investments dominated pension portfolios, providing a predictable, low-risk foundation.
Three decades ago, manufacturing and agriculture dominated employment, providing a stable, community-rooted foundation.

drillQuestions.question: Central-bank reserves and ______ debt purchases dominated foreign inflows.

sentences.notes: 과거 상황을 먼저 설명하여 다음 문장의 'Today'와 대비를 설정한다. sovereign debt는 국채를 의미하는 핵심 금융 용어.
pronunciationNotes: sovereign [ˈsɒvrɪn] -- '소브린'으로 첫 음절 강세, 두 음절로 발음

sentences.original: Today, profit-seeking equity investment accounts for a record share, nearly three-fifths, of all foreign-owned American assets.
sentences.translation: 오늘날, 수익 추구형 주식 투자가 기록적 비율, 즉 거의 5분의 3에 해당하는 외국인 소유 미국 자산의 비중을 차지한다.

grammarElements.text: a record share, nearly three-fifths, of all foreign-owned American assets
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동격 삽입)

vocabulary.example: Equity markets rallied on the back of strong earnings reports.
Services now account for more than two-thirds of GDP.

expressions.usage: 특정 항목이 전체에서 차지하는 비율이 역대 최고임을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: Today, [subject] accounts for a record share, nearly [fraction], of [total]
patterns.explanation: 과거 대비 현재의 극적 변화를 분수로 구체화하는 대조 구조
examples: Today, renewable energy accounts for a record share, nearly two-fifths, of all electricity generated.
Today, digital advertising accounts for a record share, nearly four-fifths, of all marketing spending.

drillQuestions.question: Profit-seeking equity investment ______ for a record share of all foreign-owned American assets.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 Two decades ago와 대비되는 Today로 시작하여 구조적 변화를 부각한다. nearly three-fifths라는 삽입 동격이 비율을 구체화.

sentences.original: That shift matters because equities are far more sensitive to sentiment.
sentences.translation: 그 변화가 중요한 이유는 주식이 심리에 훨씬 더 민감하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: because equities are far more sensitive to sentiment
grammarElements.label: because 이유절

vocabulary.example: Investor sentiment turned sharply negative after the earnings miss.

expressions.usage: 앞서 제시한 변화의 의미를 독자에게 직접 설명하는 분석적 연결 표현

patterns.pattern: That [change] matters because [explanation of consequence]
patterns.explanation: 앞선 사실의 중요성을 독자에게 왜 중요한지 직접 풀어주는 해설적 구조
examples: That decline matters because it signals a loss of confidence among institutional buyers.
That merger matters because it would create the largest player in the industry.

drillQuestions.question: That shift matters because equities are far more sensitive to ______.

sentences.notes: 짧고 직접적인 해설문. matters because 구조는 독자에게 '왜 이것이 중요한지'를 명확히 전달하는 저널리즘 핵심 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: equities are far more sensitive to sentiment

sentences.original: When doubts emerge about the direction of policy or the sustainability of lofty stock valuations, capital can reverse course quickly.
sentences.translation: 정책 방향이나 고평가된 주가의 지속 가능성에 대한 의문이 부상하면, 자본은 빠르게 방향을 전환할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: When doubts emerge about the direction of policy or the sustainability of lofty stock valuations
grammarElements.label: when 조건/시간절

vocabulary.example: Lofty valuations left little room for disappointment.
Tech valuations have reached levels not seen since the dotcom era.

expressions.usage: 자본, 정책, 전략 등이 기존 방향에서 반대로 돌아설 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: When doubts emerge about [A] or [B], [subject] can [verb] quickly
patterns.explanation: 불확실성이 부상할 때 빠르게 반응하는 시나리오를 제시하는 조건부 구조
examples: When doubts emerge about earnings growth or regulatory direction, shareholders can exit positions quickly.
When doubts emerge about creditworthiness or governance, bondholders can demand higher yields quickly.

drillQuestions.question: When doubts emerge, capital can reverse ______ quickly.

sentences.notes: lofty는 '높은'이라는 물리적 의미에서 '고평가된'이라는 금융적 의미로 전용된 표현. reverse course는 항해 은유에서 유래.

koreanAlignment.ko: 자본은 빠르게 방향을 전환할 수 있다

sentences.original: Recent episodes illustrate the danger: during tariff-related market shocks, stocks, bonds, and the dollar all fell simultaneously, a pattern once associated mainly with developing economies.
sentences.translation: 최근 사례들이 그 위험을 잘 보여준다: 관세 관련 시장 충격 동안 주식, 채권, 달러가 모두 동시에 하락했는데, 이는 한때 주로 개발도상국과 연관된 패턴이었다.

grammarElements.text: during tariff-related market shocks, stocks, bonds, and the dollar all fell simultaneously
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 구체적 사례

grammarElements.text: a pattern once associated mainly with developing economies
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구

vocabulary.example: The earthquake and tsunami struck almost simultaneously.
The new tariffs on imported steel raised costs for domestic manufacturers.

expressions.phrase: a pattern once associated mainly with
expressions.usage: 선진국에서 나타난 현상이 과거에는 개발도상국의 특징이었음을 암시하는 비교 표현

patterns.pattern: [Evidence] illustrates the danger: [specific event], a pattern once associated mainly with [unexpected comparison]
patterns.explanation: 콜론으로 구체적 사례를 제시한 뒤 동격으로 충격적 비교 대상을 덧붙이는 구조
examples: The bank run illustrates the danger: depositors withdrew billions in hours, a pattern once associated mainly with unstable regimes.
The outage illustrates the danger: the entire grid failed for twelve hours, a pattern once associated mainly with developing infrastructure.

drillQuestions.question: Stocks, bonds, and the dollar all fell ______, a pattern once associated mainly with developing economies.

sentences.notes: 미국 시장에서 주식, 채권, 통화가 동시 하락하는 것은 통상 신흥국에서 나타나는 현상이라는 점을 부각하여 충격을 준다. 콜론과 동격 명사구가 핵심 수사 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: stocks, bonds, and the dollar all fell simultaneously
koreanAlignment.ko: 주식, 채권, 달러가 모두 동시에 하락했다

koreanAlignment.en: a pattern once associated mainly with developing economies

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: claim with evidence and shocking comparison

sentences.original: Meanwhile, the yield advantage that American government bonds hold over their peers in other major economies has narrowed sharply, reducing one of the strongest incentives for foreign capital to stay.
sentences.translation: 한편, 미국 국채가 다른 주요 경제국의 국채 대비 보유하던 수익률 우위가 급격히 축소되어, 외국 자본이 머무를 가장 강력한 유인 중 하나를 줄이고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the yield advantage that American government bonds hold over their peers in other major economies
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 주어

grammarElements.text: reducing one of the strongest incentives for foreign capital to stay
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 결과구문

vocabulary.example: The yield on ten-year Treasury bonds fell to its lowest level in months.
The gap between the two candidates has narrowed significantly.
Tax incentives attracted new businesses to the region.

expressions.usage: 비교 대상보다 우위에 있음을 나타내는 금융 표현. peers는 동급 자산/국가를 의미

patterns.pattern: The [advantage] that [A] holds over [B] has narrowed sharply, reducing [incentive]
patterns.explanation: 상대적 우위가 축소됨에 따라 유인이 약해지는 인과 관계를 표현하는 구조
examples: The cost advantage that Asian factories hold over European ones has narrowed sharply, reducing the incentive to offshore production.
The wage advantage that the private sector holds over government jobs has narrowed sharply, reducing the pull of corporate careers.

drillQuestions.question: The yield advantage has ______ sharply, reducing one of the strongest incentives for foreign capital to stay.

sentences.notes: 주어가 매우 길어(the yield advantage that ... economies) 구조 파악이 중요하다. reducing 이하는 결과를 나타내는 분사구문.

koreanAlignment.en: American government bonds hold over their peers
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 국채가 동급 국채 대비 보유하던

koreanAlignment.en: the strongest incentives for foreign capital to stay
koreanAlignment.ko: 외국 자본이 머무를 가장 강력한 유인

sentences.original: This rebalancing away from dollar assets is already creating winners elsewhere.
sentences.translation: 달러 자산으로부터의 이러한 재조정은 이미 다른 곳에서 승자를 만들어내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: This rebalancing away from dollar assets
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 포함)

vocabulary.example: Portfolio rebalancing typically occurs at the end of each quarter.

expressions.usage: 자산 배분이 특정 대상에서 이탈하는 방향으로 변화함을 나타내는 금융 표현

patterns.pattern: This [process] is already creating [positive outcome] elsewhere
patterns.explanation: 부정적 변화가 다른 곳에서는 이미 긍정적 결과를 낳고 있음을 전환하는 문단 도입 구조
examples: This migration of talent is already creating innovation hubs elsewhere.
This divestment from fossil fuels is already creating opportunities elsewhere.

drillQuestions.question: This ______ away from dollar assets is already creating winners elsewhere.

sentences.notes: 세 번째 문단의 도입문. 앞 문단의 '위기' 톤에서 '기회'로 전환하는 피벗 역할. already가 변화가 미래가 아닌 현재 진행 중임을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: This rebalancing away from dollar assets

sentences.original: Emerging-market equities rose 34 percent in 2025, outpacing developed-world benchmarks by a wide margin, and the momentum has carried into 2026.
sentences.translation: 신흥시장 주식은 2025년에 34% 상승하여 선진국 벤치마크를 큰 차이로 앞질렀고, 그 모멘텀은 2026년까지 이어지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: outpacing developed-world benchmarks by a wide margin
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 부가 설명

grammarElements.text: and the momentum has carried into 2026
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: Wage growth has been outpacing inflation for the first time in years.
The fund consistently outperformed its benchmark over the past decade.
The reform movement gained momentum after the election.

expressions.usage: 격차가 상당함을 강조하는 표현. narrow margin(근소한 차이)과 대비

patterns.pattern: [Subject] rose [percent] in [year], outpacing [comparison] by a wide margin, and the momentum has carried into [next year]
patterns.explanation: 과거 실적을 제시하고 비교 대상을 능가했음을 분사구문으로 보충한 뒤, 추세 지속을 현재완료로 연결하는 패턴
examples: Indian stocks rose twenty-two percent in 2025, outpacing Chinese equities by a wide margin, and the momentum has carried into the new year.
Clean energy funds rose forty percent last year, outpacing traditional energy by a wide margin, and the momentum has carried into this quarter.

drillQuestions.question: Emerging-market equities rose 34 percent, ______ developed-world benchmarks by a wide margin.

sentences.notes: rose(과거)와 has carried(현재완료)의 시제 전환이 '과거 실적 + 현재 지속'을 정밀하게 구분한다.

sentences.original: Currencies from Latin America to Southeast Asia have strengthened.
sentences.translation: 라틴 아메리카에서 동남아시아에 이르는 통화들이 강세를 보이고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Currencies from Latin America to Southeast Asia
grammarElements.label: 주어 (지리적 범위 수식)

vocabulary.example: The euro strengthened against the dollar for the third consecutive month.

expressions.usage: 지리적 혹은 범위적 광범위함을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Plural subject] from [region A] to [region B] have [verb-ed]
patterns.explanation: 광범위한 지리적 범위에 걸쳐 동일한 추세가 나타남을 간결하게 표현하는 구조
examples: Stocks from Berlin to Tokyo have rallied.
Bond yields from London to Sydney have fallen.

drillQuestions.question: Currencies from Latin America to Southeast Asia have ______.

sentences.notes: 매우 짧은 문장으로 글로벌 범위를 압축적으로 전달한다. from A to B 구조가 넓은 지리적 범위를 효과적으로 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: Currencies from Latin America to Southeast Asia

sentences.original: Several forces are at work.
sentences.translation: 여러 요인이 작용하고 있다.

vocabulary.example: There are powerful economic forces at work behind the price increase.

expressions.usage: 복수의 원인을 나열하기 전에 사용하는 도입 표현. 분석 구간의 시작을 알리는 신호문

patterns.pattern: Several [factors/forces] are at work
patterns.explanation: 뒤따를 다수 원인 나열을 예고하는 짧은 전환 문장
examples: Several dynamics are at work behind the housing boom.
Several trends are at work in reshaping the labor market.

drillQuestions.question: Several forces are at ______.

sentences.notes: 4단어 문장이지만 기능적으로 중요하다. 뒤따를 분석적 나열을 예고하는 '신호문(signpost sentence)' 역할.

sentences.original: A weaker dollar eases the burden of dollar-denominated debts carried by developing nations and supports commodity exports priced in the currency.
sentences.translation: 달러 약세는 개발도상국이 지고 있는 달러 표시 부채의 부담을 덜어주고, 달러로 가격이 책정된 원자재 수출을 지원한다.

grammarElements.text: the burden of dollar-denominated debts carried by developing nations
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: and supports commodity exports priced in the currency
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사구

vocabulary.example: The rate cut eased pressure on borrowers across the economy.
Commodity prices have been volatile due to supply chain disruptions.

expressions.usage: 부채를 '짊어지고 있다'는 은유적 과거분사 수식
자산이나 상품의 표시 통화를 나타내는 금융 표현

patterns.pattern: A weaker [currency] eases [burden] and supports [benefit]
patterns.explanation: 통화 약세의 두 가지 긍정적 효과를 병렬로 제시하는 경제 분석 패턴
examples: A weaker yen eases the burden of corporate debt and supports manufacturing exports.
A weaker euro eases the competitiveness gap and supports tourism revenues.

drillQuestions.question: A weaker dollar ______ the burden of dollar-denominated debts.

sentences.notes: 달러 약세의 두 가지 수혜 메커니즘을 병렬로 설명한다. carried by와 priced in이라는 두 과거분사가 각각 명사를 뒤에서 수식하는 구조에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: supports commodity exports priced in the currency
koreanAlignment.ko: 달러로 가격이 책정된 원자재 수출을 지원한다

sentences.original: At the same time, many middle-income countries have spent years strengthening institutions, building foreign-exchange reserves, and granting central banks greater autonomy, making them more attractive destinations for diversifying investors.
sentences.translation: 동시에, 많은 중소득 국가들이 수년간 제도를 강화하고, 외환보유고를 확충하며, 중앙은행에 더 큰 자율성을 부여하여, 포트폴리오를 다변화하려는 투자자들에게 더 매력적인 목적지가 되었다.

grammarElements.text: have spent years strengthening ..., building ..., and granting ...
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + 삼중 병렬 동명사

grammarElements.text: making them more attractive destinations for diversifying investors
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Central-bank autonomy is widely regarded as essential for price stability.
Diversifying investors have shifted capital toward Asian markets.

expressions.usage: 장기간에 걸친 지속적 노력을 강조하는 현재완료 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has spent years [verb-ing A], [verb-ing B], and [verb-ing C], making them [result]
patterns.explanation: 장기간의 세 가지 노력을 병렬 열거한 뒤 그 결과를 분사구문으로 제시하는 구조
examples: The firm has spent years refining its technology, expanding its network, and hiring top talent, making it the industry leader.
The city has spent years upgrading infrastructure, attracting investment, and training workers, making it a regional hub.

drillQuestions.question: Many middle-income countries have spent years granting central banks greater ______.

sentences.notes: have spent years + 동명사 구문이 신흥국의 장기적 제도 개선 노력을 강조한다. 삼중 병렬(strengthening, building, granting)이 체계적 개혁을 암시.

koreanAlignment.en: more attractive destinations for diversifying investors

sentences.original: The structural case for this rotation is also compelling.
sentences.translation: 이러한 순환 투자에 대한 구조적 논거 또한 설득력 있다.

grammarElements.text: The structural case for this rotation
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: The rotation from growth stocks to value stocks accelerated in the second half.
The data presents a compelling argument for policy reform.

expressions.usage: 일시적 요인이 아닌 장기적, 구조적 근거를 의미하는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: The [adjective] case for [trend] is also compelling
patterns.explanation: 앞서 제시한 순환적/단기적 요인 외에 구조적/장기적 근거도 강력함을 추가하는 문단 도입 구조
examples: The demographic case for healthcare investment is also compelling.
The technological case for this transition is also compelling.

drillQuestions.question: The structural case for this rotation is also ______.

sentences.notes: 네 번째 문단의 도입문. also가 앞 문단의 순환적 요인 외에 구조적 요인도 있음을 신호하며, 분석의 깊이를 더한다.

sentences.original: Emerging-market stocks trade at a roughly 40 percent discount to their American counterparts, yet they offer exposure to many of the same growth themes, including artificial intelligence, through firms in East Asia.
sentences.translation: 신흥시장 주식은 미국 주식 대비 약 40%의 할인된 가격에 거래되지만, 동아시아 기업들을 통해 인공지능을 포함한 동일한 성장 테마에 대한 노출을 제공한다.

grammarElements.text: at a roughly 40 percent discount to their American counterparts
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (가격 비교)

grammarElements.text: yet they offer exposure to many of the same growth themes
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

grammarElements.text: including artificial intelligence, through firms in East Asia
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부가 설명

vocabulary.example: European banks trade at a discount to their American counterparts.
The fund increased its exposure to emerging-market bonds.

expressions.usage: 특정 자산이 비교 대상보다 저렴하게 평가됨을 나타내는 핵심 금융 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] trades at a [percent] discount to [comparison], yet offers exposure to [same/similar benefits]
patterns.explanation: 가격은 저렴하지만 동일한 투자 가치를 제공한다는 매력적 논거를 대조 구조로 제시하는 패턴
examples: Korean chipmakers trade at a fifty percent discount to their American counterparts, yet they offer exposure to the same AI supply chain.
European luxury stocks trade at a twenty percent discount to their pre-pandemic peaks, yet they offer exposure to the same consumer recovery.

drillQuestions.question: Emerging-market stocks trade at a roughly 40 percent discount to their American ______.

sentences.notes: yet이 가격 할인(부정적으로 보일 수 있는 정보)과 동일한 성장 테마 노출(긍정적 정보)을 대조시키며, 신흥시장 투자의 가치를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: trade at a roughly 40 percent discount
the same growth themes, including artificial intelligence

sentences.original: Global growth forecasts show developing economies expanding notably faster than advanced ones, and monetary conditions look supportive: interest rates are expected to decline without a recession on the horizon.
sentences.translation: 글로벌 성장 전망은 개발도상국이 선진국보다 눈에 띄게 더 빠르게 확장하고 있음을 보여주며, 통화 여건도 우호적이다: 경기침체 없이 금리가 하락할 것으로 예상된다.

grammarElements.text: developing economies expanding notably faster than advanced ones
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 현재분사 보어

grammarElements.text: interest rates are expected to decline without a recession on the horizon
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 부연 설명

vocabulary.example: Monetary policy remained accommodative throughout the recovery.
Low interest rates create a supportive environment for equity investment.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사건이 조만간 나타날 가능성이 있음(또는 없음)을 나타내는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Forecasts] show [A] expanding faster than [B], and [conditions] look supportive: [explanation]
patterns.explanation: 전망 데이터를 제시하고 여건이 우호적임을 확인한 뒤 콜론으로 이유를 보충하는 분석 구조
examples: Trade data show Asian exports growing faster than European ones, and policy conditions look supportive: tariff barriers are expected to ease without major disruption.
Enrollment figures show online programs expanding faster than traditional ones, and regulatory conditions look supportive: accreditation rules are expected to simplify.

drillQuestions.question: Interest rates are expected to decline without a recession on the ______.

sentences.notes: show + 목적어 + 현재분사(expanding) 구문은 지각동사와 유사한 5형식 구조. 콜론 이후가 monetary conditions look supportive를 구체적으로 설명.

koreanAlignment.ko: 개발도상국이 눈에 띄게 더 빠르게 확장하는

koreanAlignment.en: without a recession on the horizon

sentences.original: For investors who spent years concentrated in a handful of American technology giants, the combination of cheaper valuations and stronger institutional resilience abroad presents a persuasive argument for broader diversification.
sentences.translation: 수년간 소수의 미국 기술 대기업에 집중 투자했던 투자자들에게, 해외의 더 저렴한 밸류에이션과 더 강한 제도적 회복력의 조합은 더 넓은 분산 투자를 위한 설득력 있는 논거를 제시한다.

grammarElements.text: For investors who spent years concentrated in a handful of American technology giants
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절 (대상 한정)

grammarElements.text: the combination of cheaper valuations and stronger institutional resilience abroad
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복합 명사구)

grammarElements.text: a persuasive argument for broader diversification
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The economy showed remarkable resilience in the face of multiple shocks.
Portfolio diversification helps reduce exposure to any single market.
A handful of companies now control the majority of the market.

expressions.usage: 분석적 결론을 도출하며 독자를 설득하는 표현

patterns.pattern: For [specific investors], the combination of [A] and [B] presents a persuasive argument for [action]
patterns.explanation: 특정 투자자 유형을 한정한 뒤 두 가지 요인의 결합이 행동 변화의 근거가 됨을 제시하는 결론적 구조
examples: For pension funds weary of low returns, the combination of higher yields and improving governance presents a persuasive argument for emerging-market allocation.
For families paying rising rents, the combination of falling mortgage rates and ample supply presents a persuasive argument for homeownership.

drillQuestions.question: The combination of cheaper valuations and stronger institutional ______ abroad presents a persuasive argument.

sentences.notes: 문단의 결론문. a handful of American technology giants는 FAANG/빅테크 기업을 완곡하게 지칭하며, concentrated라는 과거분사가 집중 투자의 위험을 함의.

koreanAlignment.en: investors who spent years concentrated in
a handful of American technology giants
cheaper valuations and stronger institutional resilience
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 저렴한 밸류에이션과 더 강한 제도적 회복력

koreanAlignment.en: a persuasive argument for broader diversification
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 넓은 분산 투자를 위한 설득력 있는 논거

sentences.original: None of this means the dollar will lose its status as the world's primary reserve currency overnight.
sentences.translation: 이 모든 것이 달러가 세계 주요 기축통화로서의 지위를 하루아침에 잃을 것이라는 의미는 아니다.

grammarElements.text: the dollar will lose its status as the world's primary reserve currency overnight
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어 (that 생략)

vocabulary.example: The dollar has served as the world's primary reserve currency since Bretton Woods.
The regulatory landscape will not change overnight.

expressions.meaning: 이 모든 것이 ~을 의미하는 것은 아니다
expressions.usage: 앞선 논의가 과도하게 해석되는 것을 방지하는 절제적 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: None of this means [subject] will [dramatic change] overnight
patterns.explanation: 앞선 분석이 극단적 결론으로 이어지지 않음을 미리 차단하는 절제적 도입 구조
examples: None of this means globalization will reverse overnight.
None of this means the euro will replace the dollar as the world's anchor currency overnight.

drillQuestions.question: None of this means the dollar will lose its status as the world's primary ______ currency overnight.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 도입문. 앞선 4개 문단의 분석이 '달러 몰락론'으로 과잉 해석되는 것을 차단하는 nuance 표현. None of this means는 분석적 글쓰기의 핵심 절제 기법.

koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 것이 ~을 의미하는 것은 아니다

sentences.original: No rival asset is positioned to replace it.
sentences.translation: 어떤 경쟁 자산도 달러를 대체할 위치에 있지 않다.

vocabulary.example: No rival technology has yet matched its efficiency.
The company is well positioned to benefit from the trend.

expressions.usage: 현재 대안이 없음을 단언적으로 표현하는 부정 구문

patterns.pattern: No [alternative] is positioned to replace [current dominant entity]
patterns.explanation: 현재 지배적 위치에 있는 것을 대체할 대안이 없음을 간결하게 단언하는 구조
examples: No rival platform is positioned to replace it in the near term.
No single currency is positioned to replace the dollar as the global anchor.

drillQuestions.question: No rival asset is ______ to replace it.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 절제를 강화하는 매우 짧고 단호한 확인문. 7단어로 달러의 대체 불가능성을 압축.

sentences.original: However, the greenback's dominance increasingly depends on the continued outperformance of American assets rather than on the structural advantages that once seemed permanent.
sentences.translation: 그러나 달러의 지배력은 한때 영구적으로 보였던 구조적 우위가 아닌, 미국 자산의 지속적 초과 성과에 점점 더 의존하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the continued outperformance of American assets
grammarElements.label: 목적어 1 (현재 의존 대상)

grammarElements.text: rather than on the structural advantages that once seemed permanent
grammarElements.label: rather than 대조 (과거 의존 대상)

vocabulary.example: The company's market dominance has attracted regulatory scrutiny.
The fund's outperformance was driven largely by its tech holdings.

expressions.usage: 과거의 기반과 현재의 기반을 대조하여 변화된 조건을 부각시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s [status] increasingly depends on [volatile factor] rather than on [stable factor] that once seemed permanent
patterns.explanation: 지위의 근거가 안정적 구조에서 변동성 높은 성과로 이동했음을 대조하는 핵심 분석 구조
examples: The alliance's cohesion increasingly depends on shared threats rather than on the ideological alignment that once seemed permanent.
The platform's dominance increasingly depends on user habit rather than on the technological lead that once seemed permanent.

drillQuestions.question: The greenback's dominance increasingly depends on the continued ______ of American assets.

sentences.notes: 기사의 핵심 논지. 달러의 지위가 '구조적 필연'에서 '성과 의존'으로 바뀌었다는 것이 전체 기사의 요약이다. that once seemed permanent라는 관계절이 과거의 확신과 현재의 불안을 대비.

koreanAlignment.en: the continued outperformance of American assets
the structural advantages that once seemed permanent

sentences.original: If large institutional investors continue to hedge their dollar exposure or redirect capital toward other markets, a self-reinforcing cycle could develop: a falling currency reduces the weight of American holdings in global benchmarks, prompting further selling and additional weakness.
sentences.translation: 대형 기관 투자자들이 달러 노출을 헤지하거나 자본을 다른 시장으로 방향 전환하는 것을 계속한다면, 자기 강화적 순환이 발생할 수 있다: 하락하는 통화가 글로벌 벤치마크에서 미국 보유 자산의 비중을 줄이고, 이는 추가 매도와 추가 약세를 촉발한다.

grammarElements.text: If large institutional investors continue to hedge ... or redirect ...
grammarElements.label: if 조건절 (병렬 동사)

grammarElements.text: a falling currency reduces the weight ... , prompting further selling and additional weakness
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 메커니즘 설명 + 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Many funds hedge their currency exposure to reduce volatility.
A self-reinforcing cycle of selling can drive prices far below fair value.

expressions.usage: 투자 자금의 흐름이 다른 대상으로 옮겨감을 나타내는 금융 표현
하나의 행동이 연쇄적으로 같은 방향의 행동을 유발함을 나타내는 분사구문

patterns.pattern: If [actors] continue to [action], a self-reinforcing cycle could develop: [mechanism]
patterns.explanation: 조건절로 시나리오를 설정하고, 콜론 뒤에 피드백 루프의 작동 메커니즘을 설명하는 구조
examples: If depositors continue to withdraw funds, a self-reinforcing cycle could develop: falling reserves force asset sales, prompting more withdrawals.
If consumers continue to delay purchases, a self-reinforcing cycle could develop: weaker demand reduces production, prompting layoffs and further demand drops.

drillQuestions.question: A self-______ cycle could develop if institutional investors continue to hedge their dollar exposure.

sentences.notes: 기사에서 가장 복잡한 문장. if 조건 → 자기 강화적 순환 → 콜론 이후 구체적 메커니즘(하락 → 비중 축소 → 추가 매도)이라는 3단 구조. 금융 피드백 루프의 교과서적 설명.

koreanAlignment.ko: 자기 강화적 순환이 발생할 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: prompting further selling and additional weakness

pronunciationNotes: self-reinforcing [ˌselfrɪɪnˈfɔːrsɪŋ] -- '셀프리인포오싱'으로 reinforce의 두 번째 음절 강세

sentences.original: The lesson from previous periods of dollar decline is clear: once that cycle begins, it can persist for years.
sentences.translation: 과거 달러 하락 시기에서 얻을 수 있는 교훈은 명확하다: 일단 그 순환이 시작되면, 수년간 지속될 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: The lesson from previous periods of dollar decline
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: once that cycle begins, it can persist for years
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 교훈 내용 (once 시간절)

vocabulary.example: Inflation is expected to persist well into the next year.

expressions.usage: 역사적 선례를 인용하여 결론을 강화하는 마무리 표현
돌이키기 어려운 과정의 시작점을 경고하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The lesson from [historical precedent] is clear: once [process] begins, it can persist for [duration]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 교훈을 인용하여 현재 상황에 대한 경고를 발하는 기사 마무리 구조
examples: The lesson from previous recessions is clear: once consumer confidence collapses, it can take years to rebuild.
The lesson from past currency crises is clear: once capital flight begins, it can persist for a decade.

drillQuestions.question: Once that cycle begins, it can ______ for years.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장. The lesson ... is clear라는 구조가 역사적 권위를 빌려 경고를 전달하며, once가 '일단 시작되면 돌이킬 수 없다'는 긴박감을 부여한다. 짧고 단호한 종결로 여운을 남긴다.

koreanAlignment.en: The lesson from previous periods of dollar decline
koreanAlignment.ko: 과거 달러 하락 시기에서 얻은 교훈
